mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which amplifies corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return differential when both series are in their top quartile, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a stagnant federal funds rate erode real yields and signal impending monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields positive forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and rising household income, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product indicates economic contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance levels in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of industrial production indicate robust manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels fail to predict positive 60-day returns, or the relationship becomes negative when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.
